AtomicFact.description: A single atomic fact extracted from a source.

direct_quote.description: Exact quote from source supporting this fact
direct_quote.title: Direct Quote

fact_category.description: Category: statistic, measurement, causal_link, named_entity, date_time, regulatory_threshold, standard_reference, geographic, methodology
fact_category.title: Fact Category

relevance_score.description: Relevance to the research question, 0.0 to 1.0
relevance_score.title: Relevance Score

confidence.description: Confidence that this fact is correctly extracted, 0.0 to 1.0
confidence.title: Confidence

perspective.description: STORM research perspective: Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends
perspective.title: Perspective

entities.description: Key named entities (compounds, methods, organizations, metrics) mentioned in this fact. Used by the wiki mesh entity canonicalization pass. Optional; legacy data defaults to [].
entities.title: Entities

AtomicFact.title: AtomicFact

SourceAnalysis.description: Analysis of a single source document.

source_type.description: Type: journal_article, government_report, industry_report, news, standard, patent, book, other
source_type.title: Source Type

overall_relevance.description: Overall relevance to the research question, 0.0 to 1.0
overall_relevance.title: Overall Relevance

atomic_facts.description: All atomic facts extracted from this source
atomic_facts.title: Atomic Facts

evidence_summary.description: Brief summary of what this source contributes
evidence_summary.title: Evidence Summary

SourceAnalysis.title: SourceAnalysis

system:
You are a research evidence analyst. Extract atomic facts from source material.

Rules:
1. Extract 8-15 atomic facts per source. Focus on the MOST relevant, specific, and unique claims, statistics, findings, and conclusions. Quality over quantity — prioritize facts that DIRECTLY answer the research question over tangential details. Missing a highly relevant fact is worse than including marginally relevant ones.
   CRITICAL: ONLY extract from the ARTICLE BODY TEXT. NEVER extract from: navigation menus, cookie consent banners, URL paths/slugs, abbreviation/glossary tables, author byline metadata, page footers/headers, table of contents page numbers, product category labels, or marketing taglines. If the source is mostly non-content (nav, ads, cookie text), extract ZERO facts and set source_quality to 0.0.
2. Each atomic fact = ONE specific claim with the EXACT quote from the source (minimum 10 words, up to 200 chars). A quote shorter than 5 words is NEVER acceptable — skip that fact entirely.
3. Source quality: Score each source individually on 0.0-1.0 based on:
   - Peer review status (peer-reviewed journal > preprint > report > blog)
   - Author credentials (named experts > organizations > anonymous)
   - Recency (2023-2026 > 2018-2022 > older)
   - Specificity to research question (directly relevant > tangentially related)
   Do NOT use fixed scores for source types. Vary scores based on each source's specific qualities.
4. Rate relevance to the SPECIFIC research question on 0.0-1.0:
   - 0.9-1.0: Directly answers part of the research question with specific data
   - 0.7-0.8: Provides important context or supporting evidence
   - 0.5-0.6: Tangentially relevant or provides background
   - 0.1-0.4: Marginally relevant, mostly off-topic
   Each fact must get a UNIQUE relevance score. No two facts from the same source should have identical scores.
5. Include exact numbers, dates, measurements, names, standards.
6. For EVERY quantitative finding, extract ALL of the following when available:
   - Effect size with units (MD, SMD, WMD, OR, HR, RR)
   - 95% confidence interval (e.g., "95% CI -5.54 to -3.05")
   - Heterogeneity (I-squared value, e.g., "I²=96%")
   - Number of studies/trials included (e.g., "7 RCTs")
   - Total sample size (e.g., "n=269")
   - Evidence certainty/quality rating (e.g., "GRADE: moderate", "high certainty")
   - Study duration/follow-up period
   Missing any of these when the source provides them is a CRITICAL extraction failure.
7. For review/meta-analysis papers, extract findings from EACH cited study individually.
   Prioritize pooled estimates with I² and CI over narrative summaries.
8. For each atomic fact, tag the research perspective it belongs to.
9. CRITICAL (ARCH-4): The 'statement' field must be a DIRECT PARAPHRASE of the source text.
   Do NOT infer, extrapolate, or add information beyond what the direct_quote states.
   If the direct_quote is fewer than 10 words, the statement must be equally narrow in scope.
10. QUOTE LENGTH: The 'direct_quote' field MUST be 150 characters or fewer.
   Quote only the KEY phrase, not entire sentences or paragraphs.
   This forces you to identify the most important fragment of evidence.
   BAD: "A systematic review and meta-analysis of 24 randomized controlled trials totaling 1,768 participants found that alternate-day fasting produced weight loss comparable to continuous energy restriction"
   GOOD: "alternate-day fasting produced weight loss comparable to continuous energy restriction"
   A 2-word quote like 'distilled water' can only support a statement about distilled water,
   not a full sentence about healthcare settings or bacterial contamination.
   Perspectives: Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends.
   Choose the single best-fit perspective.

Output format (return ONLY this JSON structure):
{"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://example.com", "source_title": "Study Title", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.8, "overall_relevance": 0.7, "year": 2024, "authors": ["Smith J"], "venue": "Water Research", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "E. coli was detected in 30% of tested filters after 6 months", "direct_quote": "E. coli presence in 30% of tested POU filters", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.9, "confidence": 0.8, "perspective": "Scientific"}], "evidence_summary": "Bacterial contamination in POU water filters"}]}
prompt:
Research question: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting in adults with metabolic syndrome?

Analyze the following 2 sources and extract the TOP 8-15 MOST
relevant atomic facts per source. Focus on the most specific, unique, and
directly relevant numbers, dates, measurements, claims, findings,
regulations, and standards. Quality over quantity.

Source title: Intermittent Fasting App Market Disruption and Future Trends
Source type: web
Content:
Key Insights The global intermittent fasting app market size was valued at USD 292.17 million in 2025 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of 14.2% over the forecast period 2025-2033, to reach USD 824.94 million by 2033. The increasing prevalence of obesity and related chronic diseases, such as heart disease, diabetes, and stroke, is driving the growth of the market. Intermittent fasting apps provide a convenient and personalized approach to weight loss and overall health management, making them an attractive option for those seeking to improve their health and well-being. The market is segmented by application and type. The weight loss segment is expected to hold the largest share of the market, as intermittent fasting is a popular and effective weight loss strategy. The fasting manager segment is expected to witness the fastest growth over the forecast period, due to the increasing popularity of fasting for health benefits other than weight loss. Geographically, North America is expected to remain the dominant market, followed by Europe and Asia Pacific. The increasing awareness of the benefits of intermittent fasting and the growing adoption of mobile health apps are contributing to the growth of the market in these regions. Intermittent Fasting App Concentration & Characteristics The global intermittent fasting app market is highly fragmented, with numerous vendors offering a wide range of applications. Key players include Fastic, Window, Femometer, Zero Plus, Fastient, Vora, BodyFast, FastHabit, Ate, DoFasting, and Simple. Product innovation is a major driver of market growth, as vendors不断推出新功能，包括个性化饮食建议、社交支持和健康追踪。监管影响有限，因为该行业不受严格监管。 产品替代品包括传统的饮食跟踪应用程序和书籍，但由于其便利性和个性化功能，间歇性禁食应用程序正在获得越来越多的欢迎。 最终用户高度分散，来自各个年龄组、体重和健康状况。该行业并购活动频繁，新兴企业不断涌现。 Intermittent Fasting App Trends The intermittent fasting app market is experiencing rapid growth due to increasing awareness of the health benefits of intermittent fasting. Key market insights include: - 越来越多的科学证据支持间歇性禁食对体重管理、代谢健康和整体健康的好处。 - 名人代言和社交媒体影响者推广正在提高对间歇性禁食应用程序的认识。 - 智能手机普及以及移动健康应用程序的广泛采用为市场增长创造了有利条件。 - 用户对个性化和易于使用的应用程序的需求不断增长，这推动了产品创新。 Key Region or Country & Segment to Dominate the Market North America is expected to dominate the intermittent fasting app market due to a high level of health consciousness, a large population of overweight and obese individuals, and a well-developed healthcare system. Other key regions include Europe, Asia Pacific, and Latin America. In terms of segments, the weight loss segment is expected to dominate due to the increasing prevalence of obesity and related health issues. Intermittent Fasting App Product Insights Intermittent fasting apps offer a variety of features, including: - 饮食计划: Various intermittent fasting protocols, such as 16/8, 5:2, and eat-stop-eat, are available. - Personalized recommendations: Apps provide personalized饮食建议 based on individual goals, dietary preferences, and health status. - Tracking and monitoring: Users can track their fasting hours, calorie intake, and weight loss progress. - Social support: Apps often include community forums and support groups to connect with other intermittent fasters. - Health integration: Some apps integrate with wearable devices and fitness trackers to provide a comprehensive view of health data. Report Coverage & Deliverables The comprehensive intermittent fasting app market report provides detailed insights into the market, including: - Market size and growth forecasts - Competitive landscape and leading players - Key market trends and growth drivers - Regional market analysis - Product segmentation and market share - Strategic recommendations for market participants Driving Forces: What's Propelling the Intermittent Fasting App The intermittent fasting app market is being driven by several factors, including: - Rising prevalence of obesity and related health issues - Growing awareness of the health benefits of intermittent fasting - Increasing popularity of mobile health applications - Demand for personalized and convenient health solutions Challenges and Restraints in Intermittent Fasting App The intermittent fasting app market faces some challenges and restraints, such as: - Lack of long-term safety and efficacy data - Potential for adverse effects, such as electrolyte imbalances and nutrient deficiencies - Misinformation and fad diets surrounding intermittent fasting Emerging Trends in Intermittent Fasting App Emerging trends in the intermittent fasting app market include: - Integration with artificial intelligence (AI) for personalized recommendations and health tracking - Gamification and rewards to encourage adherence to fasting protocols - Focus on sustainable and healthy lifestyle changes - Partnerships with healthcare providers and insurance companies Growth Catalysts in Intermittent Fasting App Industry Growth catalysts for the intermittent fasting app industry include: - Government initiatives to promote healthy eating and weight management - Increasing investment in research on intermittent fasting - Technological advancements and innovation in mobile health applications Leading Players in the Intermittent Fasting App Keyword - Fastic: - Window: - Femometer: - Zero Plus: - Fastient: - Vora: - BodyFast: - FastHabit: - Ate: - DoFasting: - Simple: Significant developments in Intermittent Fasting App Sector - In 2022, Fastic acquired Window, strengthening its position in the market. - In 2023, Femometer launched a new intermittent fasting program with personalized meal plans and coaching. - In 2024, Zero Plus partnered with a major healthcare provider to offer discounts on intermittent fasting apps to members. Content:
1. What is the projected Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of the Intermittent Fasting App? The projected CAGR is approximately 8.2%. The Intermittent Fasting App market is anticipated to reach a value of 1,778.2 million USD by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 8.2% during the forecast period. The market growth is primarily driven by the increasing prevalence of obesity and chronic diseases, coupled with the growing awareness of the health benefits associated with intermittent fasting. Moreover, the rising adoption of mobile health technologies and the increasing availability of personalized fasting apps are further contributing to market growth. The market is segmented based on type (cloud-based and on-premises), application (men and women), and region (North America, South America, Europe, Middle East & Africa, and Asia Pacific). North America is expected to hold the largest market share, followed by Europe, due to the high penetration of mobile devices and the presence of major players in these regions. The Asia Pacific region is projected to witness the fastest growth rate, owing to the increasing awareness of intermittent fasting and the growing disposable income of consumers in this region. Key players in the market include Fastic, Window, Femometer, Zero Plus, Fastient, Vora, BodyFast, FastHabit, Ate, DoFasting, and Simple. Concentration Areas: Characteristics of Innovation: Impact of Regulations: Product Substitutes: End User Concentration: Level of M&A: Market Segmentations: Type: Cloud-based: Most popular, provides flexibility and scalability. Dominates the market with over 80% share. On-premises: Requires local installation, often used by healthcare organizations and large enterprises. Application: Man: Specific features and fasting plans tailored to male physiology. Growing segment due to increasing health awareness. Woman: Hormonal and reproductive considerations addressed. Significant market share due to extensive female user base. Industry: Health and Fitness: Provides fasting guidance for weight management, disease prevention, and overall well-being. Integration with Medical Devices: Wearable devices and medical sensors provide real-time data for personalized fasting recommendations. Personalized Nutrition: Apps incorporate genetic and dietary information to tailor fasting plans to individual needs. Community Engagement: Social features and online forums foster a sense of belonging and support.
